section.heading: Assistance to foreign countries to mitigate effects of HIV and AIDS
section.chapeau: On and after November 10, 2005, of any shipments of commodities made pursuant to section 1431(b) of this title, the Secretary of Agriculture shall, to the extent practicable, direct that tonnage equal in value to not more than $25,000,000 shall be made available to foreign countries to assist in mitigating the effects of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus and Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome on communities, including the provision of—

subparagraph.content: individuals with Human Immunodeficiency Virus or Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome in the communities; and
households in the communities, particularly individuals caring for orphaned children; and

paragraph.content: agricultural commodities monetized to provide other assistance (including assistance under microcredit and microenterprise programs) to create or restore sustainable livelihoods among individuals in the communities, particularly individuals caring for orphaned children.

note.heading: Codification
note.p: Section was enacted as part of the Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2006, and not as part of the Agricultural Act of 1949 which is classified principally to this chapter. For complete classification of the 1949 Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under section 1421 of this title and Tables.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: Provisions similar to those in this section were contained in the following prior appropriation acts:
Pub. L. 108–199, div. A, title VII, § 725, Jan. 23, 2004, 118 Stat. 34.
Pub. L. 108–7, div. A, title VII, § 725, Feb. 20, 2003, 117 Stat. 42.
